ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general kebab food product class; no specific mixed non-vegetarian kebab platter entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Meat and meat products — mixed meat dish

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard non-vegetarian kebab platter serving is approximately 300 g, comprising assorted grilled lamb, fish, chicken, and prawn kebabs arranged on a single plate.

nutrient_source.title: Non Veg Kebab Platter — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Non Veg Kebab Platter (Chef Recommendation), cr_id G00010247. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB record and is estimated at approximately 7.0 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable mixed grilled meat dishes (lamb, chicken, fish, prawn); this estimation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed grilled meat and seafood dish with low carbohydrate content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for a mixed non-vegetarian kebab platter. GI estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous category of mixed grilled meat and seafood dishes with low carbohydrate content (5.24 g/100g), consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat preparations. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (30 × 5.24 × 3) / 100 = 4.7 g, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; sodium at 1080 mg/100g will directly elevate blood pressure, and even a 150 g portion delivers over 1600 mg sodium, approaching the entire daily recommended limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100g), low GI (~30), high protein (10.7 g/100g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and high protein from grilled meats make this dish broadly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management; however, individuals should avoid sweet chutneys or dipping sauces that add sugar, and monitor saturated fat intake from lamb portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100g and estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g both exceed cardiovascular-safe thresholds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish poses a high cardiovascular concern due to very high sodium (1080 mg/100g) and estimated saturated fat (~7.0 g/100g from lamb); individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced-salt marinades, choose chicken or fish portions over lamb, and limit portion size to reduce sodium and saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb-derived arachidonic acid amplifies inflammatory pathways; sodium 1080 mg/100g worsens tissue swelling; estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g exceeds RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request chicken-only or fish-only kebabs to avoid red meat arachidonic acid, request reduced-salt marinades to limit sodium-driven tissue swelling, and be aware that bell pepper garnishes are nightshade vegetables that may trigger RA flares in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Non-Vegetarian Kebab Platter is a mixed grilled meat and seafood platter popular in Singaporean Indian and South Asian cuisine, typically served at lunch or dinner. The platter comprises assorted kebabs made from lamb, fish, chicken, and prawns, all marinated in spiced blends and grilled or tandoor-cooked to produce charred, smoky, and tender pieces. The dish is protein-rich (10.7 g/100g) with moderate to high fat (26.4 g/100g, estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100g from lamb) and very low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100g), giving it a low glycaemic index (~30) and low glycaemic load per serving. Sodium is very high at 1080 mg/100g due to spiced marinades, making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without modification. The dish is broadly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given its low carbohydrate and high protein profile, but sweet chutneys should be avoided. Clinically, the combination of high sodium and estimated saturated fat from lamb raises concern for both hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The platter is a common restaurant and catering item in Singapore, often listed as a chef's recommendation, and is typically served with mint chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010247, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous mixed grilled meat data and documented. All other per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB canonical record.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (7.0 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable mixed grilled meat dishes (lamb, chicken, fish, prawn) as the HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. This value should be replaced with a direct HPB or ICMR-NIN figure when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an exact equivalent mixed kebab platter. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is plausible for a grilled meat and seafood dish with minimal vegetable content. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (~30, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat preparations; no direct GI study exists for this specific dish. 5. Halal status is marked true as this dish is commonly prepared to halal standards in Singapore Indian restaurants, but consumers should verify with the specific establishment. 6. The dish name in the input includes a parenthetical '(Chef Recommendation)' which is a menu descriptor, not part of the canonical food name; the canonical name is normalised to 'Non-Vegetarian Kebab Platter'. 7. Aliases self-check (q8): all strings present in aliases_by_language — english: ['Non-Vegetarian Kebab Platter', 'Non Veg Kebab Platter', 'Non Veg Kebab Platter (Chef Recommendation)', 'Non Veg Kebab Platter( Chef Recommendation)', 'Mixed Kebab Platter', 'Mixed Meat Kebab Platter', 'Lamb Fish Chicken Prawn Kebab Platter', "Chef's Kebab Platter"]; chinese: ['非素烤肉拼盘', '非素烤肉拼盤']; malay: ['Piring Kebab Bukan Vegetarian']; tamil: ['அசைவ கபாப் தட்டு']; hindi: ['नॉन-वेज कबाब प्लेटर']; romanized: ['Non-Veg Kebab Platter'] — all verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
